free_text: 1.7 MeV anomaly in the 12C(p,p)12C reaction
3 MV Van de Graaff accelerator.
The energy spread within the beam is typically
Scattered protons were analyzed with a
180 degr. double-focusing magnetic spectrometer having
a mean radius of curvature of 50.8 cm for the analyzed
particles. The maximum spectrometer solid angle is
0.025 sr, the magnification is unity, and the best
resolution is dp/p=9*10**(-4). The spectrometer is
coupled to the scattering chamber through a sliding
seal of 0.32 mm beryllium copper foil. This arrangement
permits the spectrometer to be rotated under vacuum
from a scattering angle of 30 degr. on one side of the
beam to 130 degr. on the other side.
Scattered protons were deflected through a slip
system located in the focal plane of the spectrometer
and were detected with a large area solid state
detector.
Self-supporting carbon targets were prepared both by
deposition onto a glass plate from a carbon arc and by
cracking methyl iodide onto a thin nickel foil. In both
cases the carbon film was floated from its backing onto
a water surface. After reducing the surface tension of
the water by adding a small amount of detergent, the
foils could be easily lifted from the water onto thin
aluminum frames.
The relative cross sections are more accurately
determined, the errors being typically only about 3%.
SD: Small corrections in all Subents.

Statistical error in the records counts
together with uncertainties in the target
thickness, beam current integration, and solid angle
combined to give a probable error in the measured
absolute cross section of approximately 6.5%.
